rationale: Add a small procedural guard config to reduce Windows CLI argument-length risk without touching code paths; keeps payloads short by default and encourages file-based inputs.

edits.content:
{
  "schema": "brainflow.arg_length_guard.v1",
  "max_arg_chars": 6000,
  "prefer_file_payloads": true,
  "truncate_overlong_inline_payloads": true,
  "notes": "Use file-based payloads when possible to avoid Windows CLI arg-length issues."
}

risks: Guard config may be ignored if no consumer reads it; however it is non-invasive and safe.
expected_benefit: Provides a clear, low-risk directive to keep CLI args short, lowering the chance of Windows command-line length failures.
